meaning: adj.非常快的,大而壮的,极好的
examples: Brian was whirling like a dervish, slapping at the mosquitoes and moaning.
'Congratulations!' he said, slapping me on the back.
He was slapping his tambourine against his maroon wide-wale corduroys.
This was sung as well, with improvised verses about the groom that had the crowds slapping their thighs.
Albert puts Jehue through his paces again, slapping his trailing leg as he hits the hurdle.
